brands.name: Costa Oil

riskFlags.title: Pending litigation disclosed
riskFlags.detail: Franchimp's FDD-derived database notes 1 pending litigation action for Costa Oil. Nature of the action is not detailed in public summaries; prospective buyers should review Item 3 of the current FDD.

riskFlags.title: Investment can reach $1.88M against ~$221K disclosed AUV
riskFlags.detail: The land-purchase build model tops out near $1.88M per Entrepreneur/FDD, while the only public Item 19 figure is ~$221,000 AUV (2023 FDD, franchisepayback). At the high end of the range, payback economics are not supported by disclosed revenue figures.

riskFlags.title: Sharply conflicting unit counts across sources
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur (2026) lists 53 units (+278.6% 3-yr). The 2023 FDD showed 24 units (9 franchised, 15 company-owned). Franchimp's summary shows 0 franchised / 13 company-owned and 'franchising since 2022' vs Entrepreneur's 2020. The franchised vs corporate mix is materially unclear.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Older FDD/Franchise Gator cite $131,750-$212,900; range expanded as real-estate models added. Conflict flagged.

totalInvestmentMax.note: High end reflects purchased-land/new-build model; leased kiosk model far lower (~$156K-$213K).

franchiseFee.note: Confirmed by Franchise Gator and Wolf of Franchises (FDD-derived).

royaltyPercent.note: 6.5% of gross revenue, $150/week minimum (FDD fee table); confirmed by Entrepreneur 2026.

adFundPercent.note: CONFLICT: Entrepreneur and Franchise Chatter (FDD) say 2%; franchisepayback reports 4.5% advertising fund. 2% is the better-corroborated figure.

avgUnitRevenue.note: AUV from 2023 FDD per franchisepayback; sample size not disclosed. Reported operating profit ~19.3%.

item19Available.note: AUV figure attributed to FDD Item 19 by franchisepayback.

fddAvailable.note: FDD registered and summarized by multiple FDD databases.

sbaApproved.note: SBA Franchise Directory was discontinued May 2023; SBA eligibility is now lender-determined. No source verifies status.

multiUnitAvailable.note: No source explicitly confirms multi-unit/area development offering.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Exclusive marketing area of ~50,000 people or 3-mile radius; Entrepreneur also lists exclusive territories available.

ownerTypes.note: Absentee ownership not allowed; part-time operation permitted, suggesting semi-absentee may be tolerated but is not documented.

canRunPartTime.note: Entrepreneur lists part-time operation as permitted.

trainingWeeks.note: 40 total hours (20 on-the-job + 20 classroom) per Entrepreneur, approximately one week. Hours, not weeks, are the sourced figure.

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 0 employees required, which is implausible for an operating oil-change shop; treat as data caveat.

totalUnits.note: CONFLICT: 2023 FDD showed 24 units (9 franchised/15 corporate); Franchimp shows 0 franchised/13 corporate. See riskFlags.

closureCount.note: Item 20 closure detail not available in public summaries.

isGrowing.note: +278.6% 3-year unit growth per Entrepreneur, but growth is partly company-owned units.

brands.notes: Fast-growing young oil-change brand (founded 2014, franchising ~2020-2022). Key diligence items: which build model (kiosk lease vs land purchase) drives the huge investment spread, the franchised-vs-corporate unit mix, and the pending litigation noted by Franchimp.

brands.name: Big Frog Custom T-Shirts

riskFlags.title: System has been contracting since 2021
riskFlags.detail: Franchised units fell from 82 (start of 2021) to 75 (end of 2023) per Item 20 data summarized by Franchise Chatter; Entrepreneur shows 74 units with a -1% 3-year growth rate. Revenue per store is rising, but net unit count is slowly shrinking.

riskFlags.title: Veteran discount reported inconsistently
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur lists 20% off the franchise fee for veterans; Franchise Gator/FranNet materials cite a $10,000 franchise-fee discount (roughly 17% of $60K). Confirm current incentive in FDD Item 5.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Cash requirement per Entrepreneur; net worth $450,000. Confirmed by Franchise Gator.

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2024 FDD table showed $161,109-$319,684 (Franchise Chatter); Entrepreneur figure reflects newer FDD.

royaltyPercent.note: 6% of gross sales for first 6 months; thereafter greater of 6% or $1,200/month.

adFundPercent.note: Brand fund 1.5% of gross sales; PLUS required local marketing spend of greater of 5% of gross sales or $500/month.

avgUnitRevenue.note: 2023 average gross revenue across 70 franchised stores open full 12 months (2024 FDD Item 19); median $477,842. 2022 avg was $523,501.

item19Available.note: Multi-year Item 19 with average and median revenue.

fddAvailable.note: 2024 FDD reviewed in detail; 2025 review also published by Franchise Chatter.

sbaApproved.note: SBA Franchise Directory discontinued May 2023; status lender-determined.

veteranDiscount.note: CONFLICT on amount: 20% off fee (Entrepreneur) vs $10,000 off (Franchise Gator).

multiUnitAvailable.note: No source explicitly confirms multi-unit development program.

ownerTypes.note: Home-based, part-time, and absentee all disallowed; full-time owner-operator model.

trainingWeeks.note: Sourced as hours, not weeks: 146 hours on-the-job + 35 hours classroom (~4-5 weeks equivalent, not stated as weeks by any source).

employeesRequired.note: Typical staffing 2-3 people plus the owner (FranNet).

minFootprint.note: No sourced minimum square footage found.

totalUnits.note: All franchised; no company-owned units per 2024 FDD Item 20.

closureCount.note: Net decline of 7 franchised units 2021-2023 (82 to 75): -4 in 2021, -2 in 2022, -1 in 2023. Net change, not gross closures.

isGrowing.note: -1% 3-year unit growth; slow contraction since 2021 despite rising per-store revenue.

brands.notes: Mature (franchising since 2008) custom-apparel retail brand. Strongest Item 19 in this batch: $532,909 average / $477,842 median gross revenue (2023, n=70). Trade-off is a slowly shrinking system and a high effective marketing burden (1.5% brand fund + 5%/$500 local minimum).

brands.name: Magnolia Soap and Bath Co.

riskFlags.title: Investment-to-revenue ratio is thin for boutique retail
riskFlags.detail: Total investment runs $271,900-$498,500 against disclosed average gross sales of ~$304,771 (Vetted Biz from 2025 FDD). At the upper investment range, gross revenue roughly equals the buildout cost, implying a long payback for a soap/personal-care retail concept.

riskFlags.title: Royalty and unit-count figures conflict across sources
riskFlags.detail: Royalty reported as 7% (Entrepreneur, Vetted Biz) but 8% by at least one FDD summary; unit counts range from 40 franchised (2025 FDD per FDD Exchange) to 50 (Vetted Biz) to 60 (Entrepreneur 2026).
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur vs FDD Exchange vs Vetted Biz

riskFlags.title: Young system
riskFlags.detail: Franchising only since 2021; most units have short operating histories, so Item 19 averages rest on a young store base.

liquidCapitalMin.note: CONFLICT: Vetted Biz lists minimum cash of $65,000. Net worth requirement $350,000 (Entrepreneur).

totalInvestmentMin.note: 2025 FDD figure, matched by Entrepreneur and Vetted Biz. Includes $183,500-$223,000 paid to franchisor/affiliates.

royaltyPercent.note: CONFLICT: 7% per Entrepreneur and Vetted Biz; one FDD summary reports 8%. Verify in current FDD Item 6.

adFundPercent.note: CONFLICT: 2% per Entrepreneur/Vetted Biz; one source cites 1% marketing fee.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Average yearly gross sales per Vetted Biz from 2025 FDD; sample size not disclosed.

item19Available.note: Vetted Biz reports FDD-derived average gross sales, indicating an Item 19 disclosure.

fddAvailable.note: 2025 FDD on file with FDD Exchange.

sbaApproved.note: SBA Franchise Directory discontinued May 2023; status lender-determined.

veteranDiscount.note: Entrepreneur lists veterans incentive as N/A.

multiUnitAvailable.note: No source explicitly confirms multi-unit offering.

ownerTypes.note: Absentee not allowed; part-time operation permitted.

trainingWeeks.note: Five-day program at headquarters (Vetted Biz); Entrepreneur lists 32 hours on-the-job + 33 hours classroom.

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 0; implausible for staffed retail, treat as data caveat.

totalUnits.note: CONFLICT: 40 franchised per 2025 FDD (FDD Exchange); 50 per Vetted Biz; 60 per Entrepreneur 2026.

yearFounded.note: CONFLICT: Vetted Biz lists 2021 (likely franchisor entity formation). New Albany, Mississippi.

closureCount.note: Item 20 closure data not available in public summaries.

brands.notes: Fast-growing boutique natural soap/personal-care retail franchise out of Mississippi. Growth is real but the system is young (franchising 2021) and the economics (avg ~$305K gross sales vs up to $499K investment) deserve scrutiny at high-cost sites.

brands.name: DumpStor

riskFlags.title: Non-percentage royalty structure
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is a flat weekly per-dumpster fee ($19.50/dumpster/week for 1-50 units; $15 for 51+), plus $2/container/week brand fund and $40/dumpster/month required local marketing — not a percentage of revenue. One database (franchisepayback) instead reports a 6% royalty, likely from a different FDD year; verify current Item 6.

riskFlags.title: Conflicting founding/franchising dates and unit counts
riskFlags.detail: Founded 2018 + franchising 2020 (franchisepayback) vs founded/franchising 2021 (IFPG, Vetted Biz); 2024 FDD showed 11 units (9 franchised/2 corporate) while IFPG's 2026 profile shows 39 franchised + 5 company-owned. Very young, fast-changing system.
riskFlags.source: FranchisePayback vs IFPG vs Vetted Biz

riskFlags.title: Wide investment range against modest disclosed revenue
riskFlags.detail: Investment spans roughly $124K-$508K depending on truck/container fleet, while disclosed average gross sales are ~$380K (Vetted Biz, 2024 FDD). High-end fleet builds carry payback estimates of 5.6-7.6 years per Vetted Biz.

liquidCapitalMin.note: IFPG: $100K-$250K liquid; net worth $400,000. CONFLICT: Vetted Biz lists minimum cash $40,000.

totalInvestmentMax.note: High end reflects larger container/truck fleet.

royaltyPercent.note: Not percentage-based: $19.50/dumpster/week (1-50 dumpsters), $15/dumpster/week (51+). CONFLICT: franchisepayback reports 6% from 2024 FDD summary.

adFundPercent.note: Not percentage-based: brand fund $2/container/week plus $40/dumpster/month required local marketing spend.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Yearly gross sales per Vetted Biz from 2024 FDD; franchisepayback reports $387,000 AUV. Sample size not disclosed — likely very small (system had ~9 franchised units).

item19Available.note: Gross sales figure attributed to FDD; IFPG also notes financial performance data available.

fddAvailable.note: 2024 FDD on file with FDD databases.

sbaApproved.note: SBA Franchise Directory discontinued May 2023; status lender-determined.

veteranDiscount.note: IFPG notes a veteran discount is provided; amount not specified.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Royalty tiering above 50 dumpsters implies scalability, but no source confirms a multi-territory program.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Limited territory protection within zip-code-defined areas of up to 350,000 people; franchisor may adjust based on performance.

ownerTypes.note: IFPG: owner expected to be involved in all aspects of day-to-day operations.

canRunFromHome.note: Home-based opportunity per IFPG (equipment stored in yard/lot).

trainingWeeks.note: CONFLICT: Vetted Biz says two weeks at corporate HQ; IFPG says 23 hours classroom/hands-on plus optional 2-day driver training.

ongoingSupport.note: Support detail is thin in public sources.

minFootprint.note: No retail footprint; storage yard requirements not publicly specified.

totalUnits.note: 39 franchised + 5 company-owned per IFPG 2026. CONFLICT: 2024 FDD showed 11 total (9 franchised/2 corporate).

yearFounded.note: CONFLICT: franchisepayback says founded 2018 by Joe Martino (Manassas, VA); IFPG and Vetted Biz say 2021; one directory says brand roots to 2008.

closureCount.note: Item 20 closure data not available in public summaries; rapid growth phase.

brands.notes: Dumpster rental + jobsite storage hybrid (patent-pending DumpStor Pro combo unit). Per-dumpster flat-fee royalty model rewards high utilization. Young, fast-growing, B2B-focused, home-based capable. Data quality is the weakest in this batch — dates, fees, and unit counts all conflict across sources; FDD verification recommended before any customer-facing use.

brands.name: Exit Factor

riskFlags.title: Essentially no franchised operating history
riskFlags.detail: The 2024 FDD showed 4 company-owned and 0 franchised units (franchising since 2023). The brand then claims 66 new territories launched in 2025 and Entrepreneur lists 85 units in 2026 — meaning nearly the entire system has under two years of operating history, and no franchised unit results existed when most current owners bought in.

riskFlags.title: Item 19 / revenue figures conflict
riskFlags.detail: Franzy/Vetted Biz indicate Item 19 financials are included and directories circulate a ~$211K average revenue figure, but franchisepayback states the FDD 'does not disclose the average unit volume' — the $211K appears to be a third-party estimate, not an FDD-disclosed AUV. avgUnitRevenue therefore left null.

riskFlags.title: Unusual 35-year agreement term
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur lists a 35-year term of agreement, far longer than the 10-year industry norm — a long commitment to a brand with minimal track record.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Older sources (Vetted Biz/SharpSheets) cite $59,000-$82,000 from the 2024 FDD.

avgUnitRevenue.note: CONFLICT: ~$211K average revenue circulates in directories (SharpSheets estimate basis), but franchisepayback states the FDD does not disclose AUV. No FDD-verified revenue figure; left null per accuracy rules.

item19Available.note: Franzy/Vetted Biz mark Item 19 as included (likely corporate-unit data), but franchisepayback says no AUV is disclosed. Treat as Item 19 present but thin.

fddAvailable.note: FDD issued 2024-04-18 (first); 2025 FDD summarized by multiple databases.

sbaApproved.note: SBA Franchise Directory discontinued May 2023; status lender-determined.

veteranDiscount.note: 10% off franchise fee (or 5% off transfer fee) for veterans.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Master franchise plans announced internationally; domestic multi-territory offering not explicitly sourced.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Exclusive Designated Marketing Area of at least 8,000 registered businesses; performance requirement of at least 1 client per 12-month period after year one.

ownerTypes.note: Home-based consulting model; absentee not allowed. Executive-style operation plausible but not documented.

trainingWeeks.note: Sourced as hours: 20 on-the-job + 32 classroom at the Florida training center (remote option available); roughly one week equivalent, not stated as weeks.

employeesRequired.note: Staff hires require five-day training within 60 days of hire.

totalUnits.note: CONFLICT: 2024 FDD showed 4 units (all company-owned, 0 franchised); UFG announced 66 territories launched in 2025. The 85 figure likely counts sold territories, not operating units.

yearFounded.note: CONFLICT: Vetted Biz describes concept as launched 2022. Part of United Franchise Group's Starpoint Brands division.

yearFranchising.note: First FDD issued April 2024 per Vetted Biz; began offering franchises 2023.

closureCount.note: System too young for meaningful closure history.

isGrowing.note: 66 new territories launched in 2025; international expansion announced for Canada, Ireland, UK.

brands.notes: Business-exit/value-acceleration consulting franchise from United Franchise Group (sibling to Transworld Business Advisors). Low cost of entry and strong UFG infrastructure, but buyers are purchasing into a system where nearly all territories are under two years old and no FDD-verified franchisee revenue exists.

brands.name: BooXkeeping

riskFlags.title: High ongoing fee load for a B2B service
riskFlags.detail: 10% royalty plus 2% marketing fee on gross revenue is at the top of the range for bookkeeping/B2B service franchises, materially affecting net margins on a sub-$300K revenue base.

riskFlags.title: Item 19 revenue figures conflict sharply
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz reports average yearly gross sales of $157,402 from the 2025 FDD, while 1851 Franchise reports average gross annual revenue of $281,915 (and $172,078 average gross profit) also citing the 2025 FDD. Sample sizes undisclosed and necessarily tiny given system size.

riskFlags.title: Very small system with conflicting unit counts
riskFlags.detail: Unit counts range from 4-5 (older Vetted Biz/sharpsheets data) to 10 (Vetted Biz current) to 17 (Entrepreneur 2026, +240% 3-yr). Any Item 19 average rests on a handful of units.

liquidCapitalMin.note: CONFLICT: Vetted Biz lists $15,000 minimum liquid. Net worth $100,000 (Entrepreneur).

totalInvestmentMin.note: CONFLICT: Vetted Biz (2025 FDD) shows $67,546-$74,546; SharpSheets cites $38,046-$74,546. Range depends on franchise-fee tier.

franchiseFee.note: Tiered $40,000-$60,000 per Entrepreneur (likely by territory size); Vetted Biz cites $50,000 flat. Minimum tier recorded.

royaltyPercent.note: 10% of monthly gross sales; confirmed by Entrepreneur.

adFundPercent.note: 2% marketing/brand awareness fee; confirmed by Entrepreneur.

avgUnitRevenue.note: CONFLICT: Vetted Biz $157,402 avg gross sales vs 1851 Franchise $281,915 avg gross revenue, both citing the 2025 FDD. Tiny sample either way. Verify against FDD Item 19 directly.

item19Available.note: Both Vetted Biz and 1851 report FDD-derived financial performance figures.

sbaApproved.note: SBA Franchise Directory discontinued May 2023; status lender-determined.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Territory-based model; multi-territory offering not explicitly sourced.

ownerTypes.note: Home-based and part-time permitted (suggests semi-absentee feasible); absentee ownership not allowed.

trainingWeeks.note: Two weeks at corporate office (Vetted Biz); Entrepreneur lists 40 hrs OTJ + 40 hrs classroom with a 12-month phased program.

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 0-2 employees; can start solo.

totalUnits.note: CONFLICT: Vetted Biz currently shows 10 US locations; older data showed 4-5.

yearFounded.note: CONFLICT: Vetted Biz lists 2013. HQ Las Vegas, NV (originally San Diego).

closureCount.note: Item 20 closure data not available in public summaries.

isGrowing.note: +240% 3-year growth from a very small base.

brands.notes: Low-cost, home-based outsourced bookkeeping franchise. Attractive entry point and recurring-revenue model, but the system is tiny, the published revenue figures disagree by ~$125K, and the 12% combined fee load is steep. Best presented with explicit small-sample caveats.

brands.name: Travelin' Tom's Coffee

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 at a ~$200K-$260K price point
riskFlags.detail: The 2025 FDD contains no financial performance representations — franchisee earnings cannot be verified from disclosure documents despite a quarter-million-dollar all-in investment. Buyers must rely on validation calls with existing operators.

riskFlags.title: Flat-fee (non-percentage) royalty structure
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is a fixed annual fee per truck: $3,000 (years 1-2), $4,000 (years 3-6), $5,000 (years 7-10), plus ~$1,000/year ad fee — favorable for high-revenue operators, but it also means the franchisor's revenue does not depend on franchisee sales performance.

riskFlags.title: Investment range conflict between Entrepreneur and franchisor
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur lists $155,231-$171,950 while the franchisor's own site lists $201,620-$259,625 (truck alone ~$171,950). The Entrepreneur figure appears to exclude working capital and ancillary costs; franchisor figure used.

liquidCapitalMin.note: Franchisor states $30,000-$40,000 liquid. CONFLICT: Entrepreneur lists $20,000; Vetted Biz $45,000.

totalInvestmentMin.note: Franchisor's own figure; truck is ~$171,950 of it. CONFLICT: Entrepreneur lists $155,231-$171,950.

totalInvestmentMax.note: FDD databases cite $201,840-$255,325 (2025 FDD), consistent with franchisor range.

adFundPercent.note: Not percentage-based: ~$1,000/year ad royalty plus $600/year technology fee per Entrepreneur.

avgUnitRevenue.note: No Item 19 in the 2025 FDD; no FDD-verified revenue figure exists.

item19Available.note: Confirmed by Coffee Franchise Hub: no earnings disclosure in Item 19 of the 2025 FDD.

fddAvailable.note: Franchisor entity is Mobile Coffee Company (Florence, KY); 2025 FDD on file.

sbaApproved.note: SBA Franchise Directory discontinued May 2023; franchisor offers financing via strategic lenders.

veteranDiscount.note: Entrepreneur lists veteran incentives as not applicable; single-source.

multiUnitAvailable.note: Franchise fee and royalty are charged per truck; multi-truck ownership is part of the Kona Ice/Travelin' Tom's playbook.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Territories of 80,000-100,000 population; availability varies by region.

ownerTypes.note: Entrepreneur lists absentee ownership allowed; mobile event-based model commonly run semi-absentee.

trainingWeeks.note: 4-day 'Tom's Coffee Academy' in Florence, KY (free for one attendee incl. travel); Entrepreneur lists 14 hrs classroom + 10 hrs OTJ.

totalUnits.note: 2025 FDD showed 261 (258 franchised, 3 company-owned) per franchisepayback.

yearFounded.note: Launched by Kona Ice founder Tony Lamb, named for his father Tom. Some databases list 2021.

closureCount.note: Item 20 transfer/closure detail not available in public summaries.

isGrowing.note: +1,200% 3-year growth; named Entrepreneur's #1 New & Emerging Franchise; 150 trucks at end of 2023 with 300+ reservations.

brands.notes: Mobile coffee-truck sibling of Kona Ice (Mobile Coffee Company, Florence KY; founder Tony Lamb). Hyper-growth, flat-fee royalties, absentee-friendly, home-based. The single biggest diligence gap is the absence of any Item 19 earnings data — all revenue claims are anecdotal.

brands.name: Crispy Cones

riskFlags.title: High investment vs thin, tiny-sample Item 19
riskFlags.detail: Investment of $350,650-$594,900 against disclosed average revenue of roughly $229K-$251K, with FranDB reporting the Item 19 average/median of $229K based on a sample of just 2 units. Unit economics are effectively unproven at this price point.

riskFlags.title: Very young system; unit counts conflict
riskFlags.detail: 2025 FDD showed 8 units (5 franchised, 3 company-owned) while Entrepreneur's 2026 profile lists 16 units (+700% 3-yr). Shark Tank exposure (Barbara Corcoran deal) drives franchise demand that outpaces the operating track record.

riskFlags.title: Ad fund reported at 1%, 2%, and 5% across sources
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur lists a 2% ad royalty; Franchise Gator/FDD summaries say 2% authorized but currently collected at 1%; franchisepayback reports 5%. Verify Item 6 of the current FDD.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur vs Franchise Gator vs FranchisePayback

totalInvestmentMin.note: Matches 2025 FDD range cited by FranDB ($350,650-$594,900).

franchiseFee.note: Confirmed by FDD Exchange and franchisepayback.

royaltyPercent.note: 5% of gross sales; confirmed by Entrepreneur and Franchise Gator.

adFundPercent.note: CONFLICT: 2% (Entrepreneur), '2% authorized / 1% currently collected' (Franchise Gator), 5% (franchisepayback). Plus 1% technology fee per Entrepreneur.

avgUnitRevenue.note: CONFLICT: franchisepayback AUV $251,000 (2025 FDD) vs FranDB avg/median $229,000 from a 2-unit sample. Either way the sample is extremely thin.

item19Available.note: Item 19 disclosed but based on as few as 2 reporting units.

fddAvailable.note: 2025 FDD on file with FDD Exchange.

sbaApproved.note: SBA Franchise Directory discontinued May 2023; status lender-determined.

veteranDiscount.note: Entrepreneur lists veterans incentive as not available.

multiUnitAvailable.note: No source explicitly confirms multi-unit development offering.

exclusiveTerritories.note: Protected territory around store; franchisor will not open another store within it while compliant. Catering and online sales are NOT territory-restricted.

ownerTypes.note: Absentee not allowed; part-time listed as permitted by Entrepreneur.

canRunPartTime.note: Entrepreneur lists part-time as permitted; unusual for a staffed QSR-style dessert store.

trainingWeeks.note: Two weeks (nine instruction days) at a company-owned store in Rexburg, Idaho, for two people; up to two weeks additional on-site opening support.

employeesRequired.note: Entrepreneur lists 6-18 employees per location; highest staffing requirement in this batch.

totalUnits.note: CONFLICT: 2025 FDD showed 8 units (5 franchised, 3 company-owned) per franchisepayback.

yearFounded.note: Founded in Rexburg, Idaho; appeared on Shark Tank (Barbara Corcoran deal).

closureCount.note: System too young for meaningful closure history; none reported.

brands.notes: Shark Tank-backed (Barbara Corcoran) European-style grilled-dough ice cream cone concept from Rexburg, Idaho. Strong brand buzz but the riskiest economics in this batch: ~$350K-$595K to build against a $229K-$251K average revenue figure drawn from as few as 2 units.
